query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = '${user_email}' AND password = '${password}';

ground_truth_issues.description: Interpolating user_email and password directly into the SQL creates a SQL injection vulnerability.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, email, role FROM users WHERE email = ? AND password_hash = ?;

ground_truth_issues.description: SELECT * returns sensitive columns such as password hashes that the login flow does not need.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT id, email, role FROM users WHERE email = ? AND password_hash = ?;

query: SELECT id, email FROM customers UNION SELECT id, secret_value FROM admin_secrets;
context: Prepare a data export for a customer marketing campaign.

ground_truth_issues.description: The UNION includes admin_secrets and leaks privileged data into a customer-facing export.
The query mixes unrelated datasets with incompatible semantics, producing an invalid export.

query: SELECT c.id, c.full_name, c.ssn, c.email, t.subject FROM customers c JOIN support_tickets t ON t.customer_id = c.id WHERE t.status = 'open';
context: Show open support tickets to an agent dashboard.

ground_truth_issues.description: The dashboard query exposes SSNs even though the ticket workflow only needs identity and ticket context.
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT c.id, c.full_name, c.email, t.subject FROM customers c JOIN support_tickets t ON t.customer_id = c.id WHERE t.status = 'open';

query: SELECT e1.department_id, e1.id, COUNT(e2.salary) + 1 AS salary_rank FROM employees e1 LEFT JOIN employees e2 ON e1.department_id = e2.department_id AND e2.salary > e1.salary GROUP BY e1.department_id, e1.id;
context: Rank employees by salary within each department.

ground_truth_issues.description: The self-join ranking pattern is expensive and should use a window function such as DENSE_RANK().
ground_truth_issues.fix: SELECT department_id, id, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department_id ORDER BY salary DESC) AS salary_rank FROM employees;

query: UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE user_id = 10; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE user_id = 11;
context: Transfer money between two account balances.

ground_truth_issues.description: The transfer uses two updates without a transaction, so a partial failure can corrupt balances.
ground_truth_issues.fix: BEGIN; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE user_id = 10 AND balance >= 100; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE user_id = 11; COMMIT;

ground_truth_issues.description: The debit statement does not verify sufficient funds before subtracting the balance.
ground_truth_issues.fix: BEGIN; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 100 WHERE user_id = 10 AND balance >= 100; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance + 100 WHERE user_id = 11; COMMIT;

query: UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 500 WHERE user_id = 42 AND balance >= 500;
context: Deduct $500 from user account for a withdrawal. Multiple withdrawal requests may arrive concurrently.

ground_truth_issues.description: Without SELECT FOR UPDATE or proper transaction isolation, concurrent requests can pass the balance check simultaneously, allowing double-spending.
ground_truth_issues.fix: BEGIN; SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE user_id = 42 FOR UPDATE; UPDATE accounts SET balance = balance - 500 WHERE user_id = 42 AND balance >= 500; COMMIT;
